text.title: Backlog Health Principles and Structure
text.summary: DEEP and INVEST principles for backlog quality, four-tier backlog structure, and criteria for a well-formed backlog item.

rule.statement: Apply DEEP to the backlog as a whole: top items Detailed, bottom items Emergent, all items Estimated, all items Prioritized.
rule.rationale: A backlog uniformly detailed at all depths wastes refinement effort on items that will never be built. DEEP matches detail depth to proximity to execution.

rule.statement: Apply INVEST to every individual item before marking it Ready: Independent, Negotiable, Valuable, Estimable, Small, Testable.
rule.rationale: Items that fail INVEST block sprint planning — engineers cannot estimate or test them without more work, causing last-minute scope changes.

list.item: Ready (Next Sprint) — Detailed, estimated, acceptance criteria present, no blockers
Upcoming (1-3 sprints) — Defined, some detail, rough estimate
Backlog (Future) — Ideas, rough description, not yet refined
Icebox (Maybe/Later) — Parked, low priority, revisit quarterly

rule.statement: Maintain 2-4 sprints worth of Ready items, fewer than 100 total items (or grouped), and fewer than 10% stale items.
rule.rationale: These three targets are the minimal health indicators. Below 2 Ready sprints causes planning panic; above 100 ungrouped items means signal is buried in noise; above 10% stale means the backlog is a graveyard.

section.title: Backlog Item Quality

rule.statement: Every Ready item must have: clear user value, acceptance criteria (Given/When/Then format), size estimate, priority rank, and documented dependencies or none.
rule.rationale: Items reaching sprint planning without acceptance criteria cause in-sprint scope debates. Given/When/Then forces specificity that "user can do X" does not.

antipattern.description: Vague item title like "Better search" or "Fix dashboard".
antipattern.reason: Vague titles require a conversation to decode intent. Reformulate as "Add autocomplete to product search" — action-oriented, scoped, estimable.

example.note: Properly refined backlog item after grooming
example.code:
Title: Add autocomplete to product search As a customer I want to see suggestions as I type So that I find products faster
Acceptance Criteria: - Given 2+ characters typed, when user pauses 300ms, then top 5 suggestions appear - Given a suggestion is shown, when user clicks it, then product detail page opens - Given mobile viewport, when autocomplete is active, then keyboard does not obscure results
Estimate: M (3-5 days) Priority: P2 — high-usage path

section.reference: Backlog item template with full story format, AC, estimate, priority, and links.
